title: Nightly Salesforce import truncates country codes longer than 2 digits
problem: Since approximately two weeks ago, the nightly Salesforce batch import is truncating phone number country codes that are longer than 2 digits. Numbers with 1- or 2-digit country codes (US +1, UK +44) import correctly, but 3-digit country codes (e.g., Ireland +353) are truncated to 2 digits (+35), corrupting approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records. The source data in Salesforce remains correct.
root_cause_hypothesis: A recent change to the import process (described as 'supporting international phone formats better') likely introduced a fixed-length assumption or off-by-one error in the country code parsing logic, limiting country codes to a maximum of 2 digits. This would explain why +1 and +44 are fine but +353 is truncated to +35.
reproduction_steps: Identify the recent commit(s) to the Salesforce import process related to international phone format support (~2 weeks ago)
Set up a test Salesforce record with a 3-digit country code phone number (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland)
Run the nightly import process (or its equivalent in a test environment)
Verify the imported phone number in TaskFlow — expect the country code to be truncated to 2 digits
environment: Production nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow batch import. Affects international numbers with 3+ digit country codes. US and UK numbers unaffected.
impact: Approximately 50-100 customer records have corrupted phone numbers, causing support team to reach wrong numbers. Data integrity issue affecting customer communication. Ongoing — each nightly sync may re-corrupt any manually fixed records.
recommended_fix: 1. Review the recent international phone format changes to the Salesforce import pipeline, specifically the country code parsing logic. Look for hardcoded length limits, substring operations, or regex patterns that assume country codes are at most 2 digits. 2. Fix the parser to handle 1-, 2-, and 3-digit country codes per the ITU E.164 standard. 3. Run a one-time remediation to re-import affected records from Salesforce to restore correct phone numbers.
proposed_test_case: Unit test the phone number parser with representative country codes of each length: +1 (US, 1 digit), +44 (UK, 2 digits), +353 (Ireland, 3 digits), and +1-868 (Trinidad, +1 with 3-digit area code). Assert that the full country code and remaining digits are preserved exactly. Integration test: import a batch containing all three country code lengths and verify no truncation.
information_gaps: Exact commit or PR that modified the import process — dev team can identify this from their git history around 2 weeks ago
Which specific Caribbean country codes are affected — would further confirm the 3-digit truncation pattern vs. +1-area-code Caribbean numbers
Whether the nightly import overwrites previously correct data on each run (determines if manual fixes are being re-corrupted)
